basic_details.job_title: RPSC Physiotherapist Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 20 Posts
basic_details.short_information: This section gives a quick snapshot of the recruitment before you dive into the detailed eligibility and exam information.

application_fee.details: The application fee is charged once under Rajasthan's One-Time Registration (OTR) fee structure and depends on the candidate's category. | Note: Candidates who have already completed One-Time Registration under the SSO portal are not required to pay this fee again for this recruitment.

age_limit.details: Age eligibility is one of the most searched details for this recruitment, so here is the exact criterion along with relaxations available to reserved categories. | Minimum Age: 18 years Maximum Age: 40 years Age Reckoned As On: 1 January 2027 | Additional relaxations also apply to categories such as ex-prisoners, cadet instructors, emergency commissioned officers, and persons repatriated from Pakistan, Burma, Ceylon, or East African countries - as detailed in the official notification.

qualification.details: Before applying, candidates should confirm they meet the minimum academic requirement fixed by RPSC for this post. | To be eligible for the Physiotherapist (NSA) post, a candidate must possess: | Secondary examination (or its equivalent) along with a Diploma in Physiotherapy from an institution recognized by the Government. Working knowledge of Hindi written in Devanagari script along with knowledge of Rajasthani culture. | Candidates who are in the final year of the required qualifying examination may also apply, provided they submit proof of having acquired the qualification at the time prescribed by the Commission.

selection_process: Selection for the RPSC Physiotherapist post is based purely on the candidate's performance in the written examination described above. There is no interview stage mentioned in this recruitment notification. Final merit will be prepared according to marks scored
subject to eligibility verification and document scrutiny.

exam_pattern.details: Understanding the exam pattern helps candidates plan their preparation strategy for the written test. | The selection for the Physiotherapist post will be made through a written competitive examination consisting of objective/multiple-choice questions. | The question paper will have five options (1 to 5) for each question; candidates must darken only one circle using a blue ball point pen. Marking option '5' is compulsory if a candidate chooses not to attempt a question. If none of the five circles is darkened, one-third (1/3) marks will be deducted for that question. Negative marking of 1/3 applies for every wrong answer. Candidates who fail to darken any circle in more than 10% of the questions will be disqualified. An extra 10 minutes beyond the scheduled time is provided for darkening the OMR sheet. The detailed syllabus will be released separately by the Commission before the exam.

how_to_apply: Applying correctly and on time is essential to avoid rejection of the application form. Follow the steps below to complete the process.
Visit the official RPSC website: https://rpsc.rajasthan.gov.in
Alternatively, log in through the SSO portal: https://sso.rajasthan.gov.in
Complete One Time Registration (OTR) if not already done, using Aadhaar Card/Jan Aadhaar details, name, father's name, date of birth, and gender.
Log in to Citizen Apps (G2C) and select the Recruitment Portal.
Fill in the remaining personal, educational, and category-related details.
Upload a live photo captured through webcam at the time of application (photo capture through OTP-based verification is mandatory).
Choose the exam center preference (Scribe facility option, if applicable, must also be selected at this stage).
Preview the application form carefully before final submission.
Submit the form and take a printout for future reference.
Important: The application will be considered incomplete unless a valid Application Number/ID is generated after submission.
If a candidate needs to correct any details after submission, RPSC allows an online correction facility within 10 days from the last date of application, on payment of a correction fee of Rs. 500/-.

content_sections.heading: RPSC Physiotherapist Recruitment 2026: Overview
content_sections.text: This section gives a quick snapshot of the recruitment before you dive into the detailed eligibility and exam information.

content_sections.heading: RPSC Physiotherapist Recruitment 2026 Notification
content_sections.text: Download RPSC Physiotherapist Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: RPSC Physiotherapist Vacancy Details: Category-Wise Breakup
content_sections.text: Candidates often want to know how the 20 posts are distributed among different reservation categories. The table below gives the category-wise split as published in the official notification. Within these 20 posts, horizontal reservation is also provided as follows: Ex-Servicemen: General/UR - 01, OBC - 01 Persons with Disabilities (PH): LD/CP & Others category - 01 (General), 01 (OBC)

content_sections.heading: Educational Qualification for RPSC Physiotherapist Post
content_sections.text: Before applying, candidates should confirm they meet the minimum academic requirement fixed by RPSC for this post. To be eligible for the Physiotherapist (NSA) post, a candidate must possess: Secondary examination (or its equivalent) along with a Diploma in Physiotherapy from an institution recognized by the Government. Working knowledge of Hindi written in Devanagari script along with knowledge of Rajasthani culture. Candidates who are in the final year of the required qualifying examination may also apply, provided they submit proof of having acquired the qualification at the time prescribed by the Commission.

content_sections.heading: Age Limit for RPSC Physiotherapist Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Age eligibility is one of the most searched details for this recruitment, so here is the exact criterion along with relaxations available to reserved categories. Minimum Age: 18 years Maximum Age: 40 years Age Reckoned As On: 1 January 2027 Additional relaxations also apply to categories such as ex-prisoners, cadet instructors, emergency commissioned officers, and persons repatriated from Pakistan, Burma, Ceylon, or East African countries - as detailed in the official notification.

content_sections.heading: RPSC Physiotherapist Application Fee 2026
content_sections.text: The application fee is charged once under Rajasthan's One-Time Registration (OTR) fee structure and depends on the candidate's category. Note: Candidates who have already completed One-Time Registration under the SSO portal are not required to pay this fee again for this recruitment.

rows.category: General/Unreserved & Creamy Layer of reserved categories

content_sections.heading: How to Apply Online for RPSC Physiotherapist Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Applying correctly and on time is essential to avoid rejection of the application form. Follow the steps below to complete the process. Visit the official RPSC website: https://rpsc.rajasthan.gov.in Alternatively, log in through the SSO portal: https://sso.rajasthan.gov.in Complete One Time Registration (OTR) if not already done, using Aadhaar Card/Jan Aadhaar details, name, father's name, date of birth, and gender. Log in to Citizen Apps (G2C) and select the Recruitment Portal. Fill in the remaining personal, educational, and category-related details. Upload a live photo captured through webcam at the time of application (photo capture through OTP-based verification is mandatory). Choose the exam center preference (Scribe facility option, if applicable, must also be selected at this stage). Preview the application form carefully before final submission. Submit the form and take a printout for future reference. Important: The application will be considered incomplete unless a valid Application Number/ID is generated after submission. If a candidate needs to correct any details after submission, RPSC allows an online correction facility within 10 days from the last date of application, on payment of a correction fee of Rs. 500/-.

content_sections.heading: RPSC Physiotherapist Exam Pattern 2026
content_sections.text: Understanding the exam pattern helps candidates plan their preparation strategy for the written test. The selection for the Physiotherapist post will be made through a written competitive examination consisting of objective/multiple-choice questions. The question paper will have five options (1 to 5) for each question; candidates must darken only one circle using a blue ball point pen. Marking option '5' is compulsory if a candidate chooses not to attempt a question. If none of the five circles is darkened, one-third (1/3) marks will be deducted for that question. Negative marking of 1/3 applies for every wrong answer. Candidates who fail to darken any circle in more than 10% of the questions will be disqualified. An extra 10 minutes beyond the scheduled time is provided for darkening the OMR sheet. The detailed syllabus will be released separately by the Commission before the exam.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection for the RPSC Physiotherapist post is based purely on the candidate's performance in the written examination described above. There is no interview stage mentioned in this recruitment notification. Final merit will be prepared according to marks scored, subject to eligibility verification and document scrutiny.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are announced in RPSC Physiotherapist Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: RPSC has announced a total of 20 vacancies for the post of Physiotherapist (Non-Scheduled Area) across General, SC, ST, OBC, MBC, and EWS categories.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for RPSC Physiotherapist 2026?
faq.answer: The online application process closes on 21 August 2026 at 11:59 PM. Applications must be submitted through rpsc.rajasthan.gov.in or the SSO portal.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for RPSC Physiotherapist post?
faq.answer: Candidates need a Secondary (or equivalent) qualification along with a Diploma in Physiotherapy from a government-recognized institution, plus working knowledge of Hindi in Devanagari script and Rajasthani culture.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for RPSC Physiotherapist Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 40 years, calculated as on 1 January 2027. Age relaxation is available for reserved categories, women, ex-servicemen, and other specified groups.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for RPSC Physiotherapist 2026?
faq.answer: The one-time registration fee is Rs. 600 for general/unreserved candidates and Rs. 400 for reserved category and PwD candidates.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for RPSC Physiotherapist recruitment?
faq.answer: Selection is made solely through a written objective-type examination of 150 questions carrying 150 marks, to be completed in 2 hours 30 minutes, with 1/3 negative marking for wrong answers.

faq.question: Q7. Is there negative marking in the RPSC Physiotherapist exam?
faq.answer: Yes. One-third (1/3) of the marks allotted to a question will be deducted for each wrong answer, and the same deduction applies if a candidate leaves a question unmarked without selecting the mandatory option '5'.

faq.question: Q8. Where can candidates apply for RPSC Physiotherapist Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply only online through the official RPSC website https://rpsc.rajasthan.gov.in or through the Rajasthan SSO portal https://sso.rajasthan.gov.in after completing One Time Registration (OTR).
